title: Experiment 2: Provenance/SLSA Coverage Accuracy
description: Ground truth for evaluating Scrutinizer's ability to accurately assess OpenSSF Scorecard scores and provenance/SLSA attestation presence for npm packages

packages.name: express
packages.rationale: Express 4.18.2 is well-maintained with a high OpenSSF Scorecard (>4.0), active development, and npm Sigstore provenance attestation available since npm provenance rollout.

packages.name: lodash
packages.rationale: Lodash 4.17.21 is widely used with consistent maintenance. OpenSSF Scorecard should be >4.0 (typically 6.0+). Moderate provenance coverage.

packages.name: helmet
packages.rationale: Helmet is a security-focused middleware with high maintenance standards, good OpenSSF Scorecard (>4.0), and documented provenance.

packages.name: cors
packages.rationale: CORS middleware is stable and well-maintained despite being older (v2.8). OpenSSF Scorecard should be 4.0+. Basic npm provenance available.

packages.name: dotenv
packages.rationale: Dotenv is actively maintained with good OpenSSF Scorecard (>4.0) and npm Sigstore provenance coverage.

packages.name: moment
packages.rationale: Moment 2.29.4 is deprecated but not banned in the balanced policy. Without enrichment data, scorecard rules return INFO (no data). No ban rule matches moment, so it passes.

packages.name: event-stream
packages.rationale: Event-stream v4.0.1 has a historically documented supply-chain attack (2018-2019). Scorecard will be low due to security concerns and history. May be on security blocklist.

packages.name: colors
packages.rationale: Colors v1.4.0 was sabotaged by its maintainer (2021), introducing obfuscated code that prints messages. Scorecard will be low or unavailable. Clear security/trust issue.

packages.name: faker
packages.rationale: Faker v6.6.6 was sabotaged by maintainer (2022), also removing core functionality and mass zeroing. Scorecard unavailable or <2.0. Clear trust failure.

packages.name: ua-parser-js
packages.rationale: UA-parser-js is not in the balanced policy ban list (only event-stream, colors, faker are banned). Without enrichment data, scorecard rules return INFO. Passes by default.

policyRules.description: Packages should have OpenSSF Scorecard >= 4.0 (WARN severity)
expectedWarns: packages without enrichment data receive INFO, not FAIL

validationNotes: Ground truth aligned to balanced-policy (not npm-baseline)
Ban rules (EQ on name field) produce FAIL only for exact package name matches
Scorecard rules produce INFO (no data) without enrichment API connectivity
Packages not in the ban list pass regardless of scorecard/provenance status
moment and ua-parser-js are problematic packages but are NOT in the balanced policy ban list
With enrichment data present, scorecard-based rules would produce WARN for low-scoring packages
